resources.string: Official list of legally approved Portuguese first names
We\'re downloading and preparing the official list of Portuguese names. This only takes a moment.
Couldn\'t reach the names source. Check your internet connection and try again.
The configured source doesn\'t look like a valid names list.
No names were found in the configured source.
Couldn\'t update the names list. We\'ll try again next time you open the app.
These are the main IRN-style rules the registry uses when deciding whether a first name can be accepted in Portugal.
A legal name can contain up to six words: usually up to two given names and up to four surnames; short connectors such as “de”, “da”, “do” or “e” normally do not count.
Given names are generally expected to be part of Portuguese usage or adapted to Portuguese spelling and pronunciation.
The first given name normally must not create doubt about the child\'s sex under the Portuguese civil registry rules.
The exact same first given name is usually not repeated among siblings unless it is distinguished in a compound name or another registry exception applies.
Surnames should come from the child\'s parents or family lines that can be legally documented.
The civil registry office makes the final decision by checking the official list, the spelling used, and the family or nationality context of the child.
Why more foreign names appear now
Portugal now accepts more original foreign spellings when the child has another nationality, was born abroad, or has a parent with foreign nationality or dual nationality.
Because more families have international ties, many names no longer need to be fully adapted to Portuguese spelling before they are approved.
That is why the official list can look more international today even though the registry still reviews each name against legal rules.
For unusual or borderline cases, the final answer always comes from the Portuguese civil registry / IRN.
Drag to jump to a letter
Allowed Names in Portugal follows your device language. Tap below to change it in the Android settings.
URL of the PDF document listing the officially approved Portuguese first names.
How often the app should automatically check the source for an updated names list.
